segments.text: Selling skincare to kids is unethical.
But she made $100 million doing it the right way. While everybody else was complaining about Sephora kids, she was building an empire. She realized a
massive gap. Jen Alpha hates baby product. They think they're cringe and boring. So they steal their mom's expensive creams. But those products
have harsh acids that actually burn young skin. They were ruining their faces just to look cool. But Kimberly Hall saw the data. She quit her Wall
Street career and created Everden. She designed the bottle to look exactly like the expensive adult brand. Colorful,
trendy, and premium. The formula inside is 100% gentle and safe for kids. She took them and even their parents, but in the right way. She did not try to change
the kids. Instead, she changed the product. That's a $100 million insight. Subscribe for mood.
